summary: Second targeted domain re-review accepts the Opportunity Analysis Agent after the no-code negation fix. The QA no-code finding is closed from the domain/risk perspective: no-code/no code product descriptors no longer suppress output-only unsafe or regulated topic matching, while explicit safety-policy phrases such as do not build gambling tools remain ignored as policy text rather than opportunity topics.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: QA no-code finding: no-code/no code product phrasing must not bypass output-topic risk classification.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: hasNegatedPolicyCue() now uses policy-topic-aware negation patterns and explicitly excludes no-code/no code from the broad no-negation case. Output fields such as No-code Sports Betting Odds Calculator and No-code Legal Compliance Checker are classified by the unsafe/regulated topic matchers.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Explicit safety-policy mentions should not be treated as the opportunity topic.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: The implementation still ignores fields that are explicitly framed as safety policy, such as do not/avoid/cannot/must not wording near unsafe or regulated topics, so kill criteria like Do not build gambling tools do not incorrectly exclude an otherwise safe opportunity.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Regression coverage for no-code unsafe and regulated drift is sufficient for this slice.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: Tests now cover output-only no-code gambling/betting drift, output-only no-code legal/compliance drift, and a safety-policy non-regression. The full suite passes with 32 tests.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: The broader M3 product/domain boundary remains intact.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: The targeted change is limited to risk guardrail negation handling and tests. No scoring engine, scan orchestration, persistence, API, UI, provider wiring, env reads, network calls, or paid-service setup was added.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Updated implementation and QA handoffs are valid and reviewed.
acceptance_criteria_review.notes: Both handoff files validate. The latest QA handoff documents the prior REQUEST_CHANGES finding, and the implementation handoff documents the no-code negation fix and 32-test pass.

decisions: Accepted the refined negation handling because it distinguishes no-code product descriptors from explicit safety-policy negations.
Accepted the new test coverage as closing the QA follow-up scenario for output-only no-code gambling and legal/compliance LLM drift.
Maintained the previous domain acceptance for the broader Opportunity Analysis Agent slice, including cautious monetization/user-fit inference, no final scoring engine, and no persistence.
risks: Regex-based risk classification remains a conservative MVP guardrail and should keep accumulating real-provider edge-case tests before broad LLM use.
The build passes but this local Windows environment still prints nonfatal @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc native-package warnings.
Heuristic monetization and user-fit inference may need tuning after real scan data and feedback exist.

tests_run.result: PASS: no whitespace errors; Git printed LF-to-CRLF normalization warnings only.
PASS: 32 tests passed across 7 suites, including no-code unsafe/regulated drift regressions and the safety-policy non-regression.
PASS: Next.js production build completed; nonfatal local @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc warnings were printed.

requested_action: Record domain_designer acceptance on the second targeted re-review. The no-code QA finding is closed from the domain/risk perspective; no further implementation changes are requested by domain_designer.
